refnamediv.refpurpose: Inspect and manage the platformd Secret Service

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: secretctl inspects and manages platformd-secretd.service8, the platformd Secret Service provider. It is the provider's companion command-line client, in the manner of homectl1 or loginctl1.

Storing and retrieving individual secrets is the job of the standard Secret Service client secret-tool1 and of any application using libsecret; the service lifecycle is managed with systemctl1. secretctl covers what those do not: inspecting and controlling the provider itself.

listitem.para: Show the state of the Secret Service on the current session. This command is read-only: it neither claims the org.freedesktop.secrets bus name nor stores or reads any secret. It reports:
the systemd-logind.service8 session, seat, type, class and active state for the current login, and the declared lock state;
a caller-identity probe of the invoking process — its UID, PID, unit and control group, and the resulting identity-evidence grade.
List the items in the default collection — each item's label and attributes — together with whether the collection is currently locked.
Lock the default collection. While it is locked, reads of its items are refused with org.freedesktop.Secret.Error.IsLocked.
Print a short help text and exit.
Print a short version string and exit.

refsect1.title: Exit status
refsect1.para: On success, 0 is returned; otherwise a non-zero failure code.
